assertionsTested.title: HTTP transport starts
assertionsTested.reason: `spikes mcp serve --transport http --port 3848` started successfully and served HTTP responses on localhost:3848.

evidence.httpProbe: curl -i http://localhost:3848/ -> HTTP/1.1 406 Not Acceptable (expected MCP endpoint response surface)

assertionsTested.title: HTTP transport serves MCP
assertionsTested.reason: HTTP JSON-RPC session succeeded for initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, and tools/call(get_spikes).

evidence.initialize: POST / with initialize -> HTTP/1.1 200, mcp-session-id header present, serverInfo.name=spikes-mcp
evidence.initialized: POST notifications/initialized with mcp-session-id -> HTTP/1.1 202 Accepted
evidence.toolsList: POST tools/list with mcp-session-id -> HTTP/1.1 200 and tools array returned
evidence.toolCall: POST tools/call get_spikes -> HTTP/1.1 200 and text result includes fixture comment 'fixture spike for mcp-http-9900d'

assertionsTested.title: HTTP binds to localhost by default
assertionsTested.reason: Listener inspection showed binding to 127.0.0.1 only, with no 0.0.0.0 listener.

assertionsTested.title: Stdio remains default
assertionsTested.reason: Running `spikes mcp serve` without `--transport` accepted stdin JSON-RPC initialize and returned stdout initialize response from spikes-mcp.

evidence.stderr: [spikes-mcp] Starting MCP server on stdio (LOCAL mode)...

summary: Validated VAL-MCP-030, VAL-MCP-031, VAL-MCP-033, and VAL-MCP-034 in namespace mcp-http-9900d using isolated workspace /tmp/spikes-utv-mcp-http-9900d. All four assertions passed.
